links.title: E = T*H/(O+B): A Dimensionless Control Parameter for Mixture-of-Experts Ecology

papers.summary: We introduce E = T*H/(O+B), a dimensionless control parameter that predicts whether Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models will develop a healthy expert ecology or collapse into dead experts. E combines four hyperparameters -- routing temperature T, routing entropy weight H, oracle weight O, and balance weight B -- into a single quantity. Through 12 controlled experiments (8 vision, 4 language) totaling over 11,000 training epochs, we establish that E >= 0.5 alone is sufficient to guarantee zero dead e

links.title: Safactory: A Scalable Agent Factory for Trustworthy Autonomous Intelligence

primary_link.title: Safactory: A Scalable Agent Factory for Trustworthy Autonomous Intelligence

papers.summary: As large models evolve from conversational assistants into autonomous agents, challenges increasingly arise from long-horizon decision making, tool use, and real environment interaction. Existing agenticinfrastructure remain fragmented across evaluation, data management, and agent evolution, making it difficult to discover risks systematically and improve models in a continuous closed loop. In this report, we present \textbf{Safactory}, a scalable agent factory for trustworthy autonomous intelli
papers.summary_zh: 隨著 large models 從 conversational assistants 演變為 autonomous agents，長期決策 (long-horizon decision making)、工具使用 (tool use) 和真實環境互動 (real environment interaction) 帶來了越來越多的挑戰。現有的 agent infrastructure 在評估 (evaluation)、數據管理 (data management) 和 agent 演進 (agent evolution) 方面仍然支離破碎，使得系統性地發現風險並在連續的 closed loop 中改進模型變得困難。在本報告中，我們介紹了 **Safactory**，一個用於可信賴自主智慧的可擴展 agent factory。

links.title: Memory Inception: Latent-Space KV Cache Manipulation for Steering LLMs

primary_link.title: Memory Inception: Latent-Space KV Cache Manipulation for Steering LLMs

papers.summary: Steering large language models (LLMs) is usually done by either instruction prompting or activation steering. Prompting often gives strong control, but caches guidance tokens at every layer and can clutter long interactions; activation steering is compact but typically weaker and does not support large structured reminders. We introduce memory inception (MI), a training-free method that steers in latent attention space by inserting text-derived key-value (KV) banks only at selected layers. Rathe
papers.summary_zh: 引導 large language models (LLMs) 通常透過 instruction prompting 或 activation steering 來完成。Prompting 通常能提供強大的控制，但會在每一層快取 (caches) guidance tokens 並可能使長期互動變得混亂；activation steering 則較為緊湊但通常較弱，且不支持大型結構化的提醒。我們引入了 Memory Inception (MI)，這是一種無需訓練的方法，透過僅在選定層插入文本衍生的 key-value (KV) banks，在 latent attention space 中進行引導。

primary_link.title: Beyond Fixed Benchmarks and Worst-Case Attacks: Dynamic Boundary Evaluation for Language Models

papers.summary: Evaluating large language models (LLMs) today rests on fixed benchmarks that apply the same set of items to any model, producing ceiling and floor effects that mask capability gaps. We argue that the most informative evaluation signal lies at the boundary, where the per-prompt pass probability is near $0.5$ under random-sampling decoding, and propose Dynamic Boundary Evaluation (DBE), which actively locates each model's boundary and places it on a globally comparable difficulty scale. DBE delive
papers.summary_zh: 當今評估 large language models (LLMs) 依賴於固定的 benchmarks，這些 benchmarks 對任何模型應用相同的項目集，產生遮蔽能力差距的 ceiling and floor effects。我們認為最具資訊性的評估信號位於邊界，即在 random-sampling decoding 下，每個 prompt 的通過機率接近 $0.5$，並提出了 Dynamic Boundary Evaluation (DBE)，它主動定位每個模型的邊界並將其置於一個全球可比較的難度尺度上。

Mixture-of-Experts (MoEs) seemingly offer a potential alternative by activating only a subset of experts per input, but in practice, restricting inference to a subset of experts for a given domain leads to severe performance degradation. links.title: Long Context Pre-Training with Lighthouse Attention

primary_link.title: Long Context Pre-Training with Lighthouse Attention

papers.summary: Training causal transformers at extreme sequence lengths is bottlenecked by the quadratic time and memory of scaled dot-product attention (SDPA). In this work, we propose Lighthouse Attention, a training-only symmetrical selection-based hierarchical attention algorithm that wraps around ordinary SDPA and can be easily removed towards the end of the training. Our hierarchical selection is also gradient-free, which exempts us from dealing with a complicated and potentially inefficient backward pas
papers.summary_zh: 在極端序列長度下訓練 causal transformers 受限於 scaled dot-product attention (SDPA) 的二次時間和記憶體消耗。在這項工作中，我們提出了 Lighthouse Attention，這是一種僅用於訓練的對稱性基於選擇的分層 attention 算法，它包裝在普通的 SDPA 周圍，並且可以在訓練結束時輕鬆移除。我們的分層選擇也是 gradient-free 的，這使我們無需處理複雜且可能低效的 backward pass。

primary_link.title: Don't Lose Focus: Activation Steering via Key-Orthogonal Projections

links.title: TIDE: Every Layer Knows the Token Beneath the Context

primary_link.title: TIDE: Every Layer Knows the Token Beneath the Context

papers.summary: We revisit a universally accepted but under-examined design choice in every modern LLM: a token index is looked up once at the input embedding layer and then permanently discarded. This single-injection assumption induces two structural failures: (i) the Rare Token Problem, where a Zipf-type distribution of vocabulary causes rare-token embeddings are chronically under-trained due to receiving a fraction of the cumulative gradient signal compared to common tokens; and (ii) the Contextual Collapse

For any first-order attribution $φ(f)$ explaining a model $f$, we measure the directional influence of feature $j$ on the attribution of feature $i$, denoted as meta-attribution $\varphi_{j \to i}(f)$, by treating the attribution method itself as a cooperative game and computing its Shapley value. Theoretically, we prove that attributions hierarchically decompose into meta-at

links.title: Profiling for Pennies: Unveiling the Privacy Iceberg of LLM Agents

primary_link.title: Profiling for Pennies: Unveiling the Privacy Iceberg of LLM Agents

papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have revolutionized how information are collected, aggregated, and reasoned. However, this enables a novel and accessible vector of privacy intrusion: the automated and in-depth personal profiling; this engenders a chilling effect of "peepers everywhere". Existing research primarily unfolds from the training pipeline of LLM, emphasizing the exposure of Personally Identifiable Information (PII) through memorization, while privacy studies from a human-centric perspecti
papers.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 徹底改變了資訊的收集、聚合和推理方式。然而，這也催生了一種新穎且易於接近的隱私入侵途徑：自動化和深入的個人側寫 (personal profiling)；這導致了「到處都是偷窺者」的寒蟬效應 (chilling effect)。現有研究主要從 LLM 的訓練流程展開，強調透過記憶化 (memorization) 暴露 Personally Identifiable Information (PII)，而以人為中心的隱私研究則...
papers.title: Profiling for Pennies: Unveiling the Privacy Iceberg of LLM Agents
papers.title_zh: 廉價側寫：揭示 LLM Agents 的隱私冰山

links.title: A$^2$TGPO: Agentic Turn-Group Policy Optimization with Adaptive Turn-level Clipping

primary_link.title: A$^2$TGPO: Agentic Turn-Group Policy Optimization with Adaptive Turn-level Clipping

papers.summary: Reinforcement learning for agentic large language models (LLMs) typically relies on a sparse, trajectory-level outcome reward, making it difficult to evaluate the contribution of individual tool-calls within multi-turn interactions. Existing approaches to such process credit assignment either depend on separate external process reward models that introduce additional consumption, or tree-based structural rollout that merely redistributes the outcome signal while constraining trajectory diversity
papers.summary_zh: 針對 agentic 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 的強化學習 (Reinforcement learning) 通常依賴稀疏的、軌跡級別的結果獎勵 (outcome reward)，這使得在多回合互動中難以評估單個 tool-calls 的貢獻。現有處理此類過程信用分配 (process credit assignment) 的方法，要麼依賴引入額外消耗的獨立外部過程獎勵模型 (process reward models)，要麼依賴僅重新分配結果訊號 (outcome signal) 並限制軌跡多樣性 (trajectory diversity) 的基於樹的結構化 rollout。
papers.title: A$^2$TGPO: Agentic Turn-Group Policy Optimization with Adaptive Turn-level Clipping
papers.title_zh: A$^2$TGPO：具備自適應回合級別裁切的 Agentic Turn-Group Policy Optimization

papers.summary_zh: 能夠可靠地區分人類撰寫的文本與大型語言模型生成的文本，具有深遠的社會重要性。解決此問題的主流方法利用了「似然假設」（likelihood hypothesis）：即機器生成的文本對檢測器語言模型而言，應比人類撰寫的文本更具可能性。然而，我們證明了區分人類和機器文本的 token-level 信號在檢測器模型的 hidden space 中並非均勻分佈，並且天真地平均似然...

papers.summary:
Evaluating and predicting the performance of large language models (LLMs) in multi-turn conversational settings is critical yet computationally expensive; key events -- e.g., jailbreaks or successful task completion by an agent -- often emerge only after repeated interactions. These events might be rare, and under any feasible computational budget, remain unobserved.

papers.summary_zh: 在多輪對話情境中評估和預測大型語言模型 (LLMs) 的性能至關重要，但計算成本高昂；關鍵事件——例如，jailbreaks 或 agent 成功完成任務——往往只在重複互動後才會出現。這些事件可能很稀有，並且在任何可行的計算預算下都可能未被觀察到。最近的 conformal survival frameworks 在觸發... 的迭代次數上構建了可靠的 lower predictive bounds (LPBs)。

We present CCL-Bench, a trace-based benchmark that addresses the limitations of
papers.summary_zh: 關於 LLM 基礎設施的評估主張——「工作負載 X 在硬體 Y 上使用軟體 Z 運行最快」——取決於一個複雜的配置空間，該空間涵蓋了硬體加速器、互連頻寬、軟體框架、並行計畫和通訊函式庫。目前的基礎設施評估基準測試發布了一小組 end-to-end 數據，但未能解釋為何一種配置優於另一種。我們提出了 CCL-Bench，這是一個基於 trace 的基準測試，旨在解決... 的限制。

links.title: ROSE: Rollout On Serving GPUs via Cooperative Elasticity for Agentic RL

primary_link.title: ROSE: Rollout On Serving GPUs via Cooperative Elasticity for Agentic RL

papers.summary:
Agentic reinforcement learning (RL) has emerged as a key driver for improving the multi-step reasoning and tool-use capabilities of LLMs. However, its efficiency is bottlenecked by long-tail rollouts with multi-turn environment interactions, making static GPU provisioning a poor fit: overprovisioning wastes GPUs on stragglers, while underprovisioning increases contention and slows training.
  We observe that production serving clusters routinely leave substantial GPU compute and memory headroom.
papers.summary_zh: Agentic reinforcement learning (RL) 已成為改進 LLMs 的多步驟推理和工具使用能力的一個關鍵驅動力。然而，其效率受制於具有多輪環境互動的 long-tail rollouts，這使得靜態 GPU 供應成為不良選擇：過度配置在 stragglers 上浪費 GPU，而配置不足則會增加資源競爭並減慢訓練速度。我們觀察到，生產環境中的 serving clusters 通常會留下大量的 GPU 計算和記憶體 headroom。
papers.title: ROSE: Rollout On Serving GPUs via Cooperative Elasticity for Agentic RL
papers.title_zh: ROSE：透過 Agentic RL 的協同彈性在 Serving GPUs 上進行 Rollout

primary_link.title: Efficient Serving for Dynamic Agent Workflows with Prediction-based KV-Cache Management

papers.summary: LLM-based workflows compose specialized agents to execute complex tasks, and these agents usually share substantial context, allowing KV-Cache reuse to save computation. Existing approaches either manage KV-Cache at agent level and fail to exploit the reuse opportunities within workflows, or manage cache at the workflow level but assume that each workflow calls a static sequence of agents. papers.summary_zh: 基於 LLM 的工作流程會組合專業 Agent 以執行複雜任務，這些 Agent 通常共享大量上下文，從而允許 KV-Cache 重用以節省計算。現有方法要麼在 Agent 層級管理 KV-Cache，未能利用工作流程內的重用機會；要麼在工作流程層級管理快取，但假設每個工作流程都會呼叫靜態序列的 Agent。然而，實際的工作流程通常是動態的，其中被呼叫的 Agent 序列以及由此引起的快取訪問模式會不斷變化。

primary_link.title: Cubit: Token Mixer with Kernel Ridge Regression

papers.summary: Since its introduction in 2017, the Transformer has become one of the most widely adopted architectures in modern deep learning. Despite extensive efforts to improve positional encoding, attention mechanisms, and feed-forward networks, the core token-mixing mechanism in Transformers remains attention. papers.summary_zh: 自 2017 年推出以來，Transformer 已成為現代深度學習中最廣泛採用的架構之一。儘管在改進 positional encoding、attention mechanisms 和 feed-forward networks 方面進行了大量努力，但 Transformer 中的核心 Token 混合機制仍然是 attention。在這項工作中，我們展示了 Transformer 中的 attention 模組可以被解釋為執行 Nadaraya-Watson regression，其中它計算 Token 之間的相似性並聚合相應的資訊。

papers.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 已經徹底改變了人工智慧，但它們的計算要求對於大多數用戶來說仍然過高。標準的推理需要昂貴的資料中心 GPU 或雲端 API 存取，導致超過十億台個人電腦在 AI 工作負載方面未被充分利用。Ternary models 提供了一條前進的道路：它們的權重被約束在 {-1, 0, +1}，理論上消除了對 floating-point multiplication 的需求。然而，現有框架未能充分利用這種結構，導致在 CPU 上實施效率低下。

Paradoxically, we observe that tool-enabled evaluation can degrade reasoning performance even when the strong thinking models make almost no actual tool calls. In this paper, we investigate how to inject natural tool-use behavior into a strong thinking model without sacrificing its no-tool reasoning ability, and present a comprehensive TIR recipe. We highlight that (i) the effe
radar.summary_zh: 工具整合推理 (Tool-integrated reasoning, TIR) 提供了一種直接方法，將思考模型 (thinking models) 擴展到文字專有推理的限制之外。矛盾的是，我們觀察到即使強大的思考模型幾乎沒有進行實際的工具呼叫，啟用工具的評估 (tool-enabled evaluation) 也能降低推理性能。在本文中，我們研究如何在不犧牲其無工具推理能力的情況下，將自然的工具使用行為注入強大的思考模型中，並提出一個全面的 TIR 方案 (recipe)。我們強調 (i) 該效
radar.title: Teaching Thinking Models to Reason with Tools: A Full-Pipeline Recipe for Tool-Integrated Reasoning
radar.title_zh: 教導思考模型利用工具進行推理：一個工具整合推理的全管線方案

links.title: When and Why SignSGD Outperforms SGD: A Theoretical Study Based on $\ell_1$-norm Lower Bounds

primary_link.title: When and Why SignSGD Outperforms SGD: A Theoretical Study Based on $\ell_1$-norm Lower Bounds

radar.summary: Sign-based optimization algorithms, such as SignSGD and Muon, have garnered significant attention for their remarkable performance in training large foundation models. Despite this empirical success, we still lack a theoretical understanding of when and why these sign-based methods outperform vanilla SGD. The core obstacle is that under standard smoothness and finite variance conditions, SGD is known to be minimax optimal for finding stationary points measured by $\ell_2$-norms, thereby fundamen
radar.summary_zh: 基於符號 (Sign-based) 的最佳化演算法，例如 SignSGD 和 Muon，因其在訓練大型基礎模型 (large foundation models) 方面的卓越性能而受到廣泛關注。儘管取得了這樣的實證成功，我們仍然缺乏關於這些基於符號的方法何時以及為何優於 vanilla SGD 的理論理解。核心障礙在於，在標準平滑度和有限變異數條件下，SGD 被認為是尋找由 $\ell_2$-範數衡量的駐點 (stationary points) 的 minimax 最優解，從而從根本上
radar.title: When and Why SignSGD Outperforms SGD: A Theoretical Study Based on $\ell_1$-norm Lower Bounds
radar.title_zh: SignSGD 何時以及為何優於 SGD：一項基於 $\ell_1$-範數下界的理論研究

links.title: FedAttr: Towards Privacy-preserving Client-Level Attribution in Federated LLM Fine-tuning

primary_link.title: FedAttr: Towards Privacy-preserving Client-Level Attribution in Federated LLM Fine-tuning

radar.summary: Watermark radioactivity testing type of methods can detect whether a model was trained on watermarked documents, and have become key tools for protecting data ownership in the fine-tuning of large language models (LLMs). Existing works have proved their effectiveness in centralized LLM fine-tuning. However, this type of method faces several challenges and remains underexplored in federated learning (FL), a widely-applied paradigm for fine-tuning LLMs collaboratively on private data across differ
radar.summary_zh: 浮水印放射性測試 (Watermark radioactivity testing) 類型的方法可以檢測模型是否在帶有浮水印的文件上進行了訓練，並已成為保護大型語言模型 (LLMs) fine-tuning 中資料所有權的關鍵工具。現有工作已經證明了它們在集中式 LLM fine-tuning 中的有效性。然而，這類方法在聯邦學習 (FL) 中面臨多項挑戰且仍未得到充分探索，聯邦學習是一種廣泛應用的範式，用於在不同
radar.title: FedAttr: Towards Privacy-preserving Client-Level Attribution in Federated LLM Fine-tuning
radar.title_zh: FedAttr：邁向聯邦 LLM Fine-tuning 中的隱私保護客戶端層級歸因

links.title: OA-WAM: Object-Addressable World Action Model for Robust Robot Manipulation

primary_link.title: OA-WAM: Object-Addressable World Action Model for Robust Robot Manipulation

radar.summary: World Action Models (WAMs) enhance Vision-Language-Action policies by jointly predicting scene evolution and robot actions, but existing methods usually represent the predicted world as holistic images, video tokens, or global latents. These representations are difficult for an action decoder to address when an instruction refers to a particular object, especially under scene shifts where object identity is entangled with context. We propose OA-WAM, an Object-Addressable World Action Model for r
radar.summary_zh: World Action Models (WAMs) 透過共同預測場景演變和機器人動作來增強 Vision-Language-Action 策略，但現有方法通常將預測的世界表示為 holistic images、video tokens 或 global latents。當指令涉及特定物件時，這些表示對於 action decoder 來說很難處理，特別是在物件身份與上下文糾纏不清的場景變化下。我們提出 OA-WAM，一種用於穩健機器人操作的 Object-Addressable World Action Model。
radar.title: OA-WAM: Object-Addressable World Action Model for Robust Robot Manipulation
radar.title_zh: OA-WAM：用於穩健機器人操作的可物件定址 World Action Model

links.title: Asymmetric On-Policy Distillation: Bridging Exploitation and Imitation at the Token Level

primary_link.title: Asymmetric On-Policy Distillation: Bridging Exploitation and Imitation at the Token Level

radar.summary: On-policy distillation (OPD) trains a student on its own trajectories with token-level teacher feedback and often outperforms off-policy distillation and standard reinforcement learning. However, we find that its standard advantage weighted policy gradient suffers from three structural weaknesses, including high variance updates, vanishing gradients in zero-advantage regions, and exploration bottlenecks when corrective signals are insufficient.We therefore propose Asymmetric On-Policy Distillati
radar.summary_zh: On-policy distillation (OPD) 透過 token 層級的教師回饋在其自身軌跡上訓練學生模型，其表現通常優於 off-policy distillation 和標準 reinforcement learning。然而，我們發現其標準的 advantage weighted policy gradient 存在三個結構性弱點，包括高方差更新、zero-advantage 區域中的 vanishing gradients，以及當糾正訊號不足時的 exploration bottlenecks。因此，我們提出 Asymmetric On-Policy Distillation。
radar.title: Asymmetric On-Policy Distillation: Bridging Exploitation and Imitation at the Token Level
radar.title_zh: 非對稱 On-Policy Distillation：彌合 Token 層級的 Exploitation 和 Imitation

links.title: Independent Learning of Nash Equilibria in Partially Observable Markov Potential Games with Decoupled Dynamics

primary_link.title: Independent Learning of Nash Equilibria in Partially Observable Markov Potential Games with Decoupled Dynamics

radar.summary: We study Nash equilibrium learning in partially observable Markov games (POMGs), a multi-agent reinforcement learning framework in which agents cannot fully observe the underlying state. Prior work in this setting relies on centralization or information sharing, and suffers from sample and computational complexity that scales exponentially in the number of players. We focus on a subclass of POMGs with independent state transitions, where agents remain coupled through their rewards, and assume th
radar.summary_zh: 我們在 partially observable Markov games (POMGs) 中研究 Nash equilibrium 學習，這是一種 multi-agent reinforcement learning 框架，其中 agent 無法完全觀察底層狀態。在此設定下的先前工作依賴於中心化或資訊共享，並面臨樣本和計算複雜度隨參與者數量呈指數增長的問題。我們專注於一類具有獨立狀態轉換的 POMGs，其中 agent 透過其獎勵保持耦合。
radar.title: Independent Learning of Nash Equilibria in Partially Observable Markov Potential Games with Decoupled Dynamics
radar.title_zh: 在具有解耦動態的部分可觀察 Markov Potential Games 中獨立學習 Nash Equilibria

links.title: Measuring Evaluation-Context Divergence in Open-Weight LLMs: A Paired-Prompt Protocol with Pilot Evidence of Alignment-Pipeline-Specific Heterogeneity

primary_link.title: Measuring Evaluation-Context Divergence in Open-Weight LLMs: A Paired-Prompt Protocol with Pilot Evidence of Alignment-Pipeline-Specific Heterogeneity

radar.summary: Safety benchmarks are routinely treated as evidence about how a language model will behave once deployed, but this inference is fragile if behavior depends on whether a prompt looks like an evaluation. We define evaluation-context divergence as an observable within-item change in behavior induced by framing a fixed task as an evaluation, a live deployment interaction, or a neutral request, and present a paired-prompt protocol that measures it in open-weight LLMs while controlling for paraphrase
radar.summary_zh: 安全基準通常被視為語言模型部署後行為方式的證據，但如果行為取決於提示是否看起來像評估，這種推斷是脆弱的。我們將 evaluation-context divergence 定義為由將固定任務構建為評估、實時部署互動或中立請求所引起的行為中可觀察到的 item 內變化，並提出一種配對提示協議，用於在控制 paraphrase 的情況下測量 open-weight LLMs 中的這種現象。
radar.title: Measuring Evaluation-Context Divergence in Open-Weight LLMs: A Paired-Prompt Protocol with Pilot Evidence of Alignment-Pipeline-Specific Heterogeneity
radar.title_zh: 測量 Open-Weight LLMs 中的評估上下文差異：一種帶有 Alignment-Pipeline 特定異質性初步證據的配對提示協議

links.title: Quantifying the Statistical Effect of Rubric Modifications on Human-Autorater Agreement

primary_link.title: Quantifying the Statistical Effect of Rubric Modifications on Human-Autorater Agreement

radar.summary: Autoraters, also referred to as LLM-as-judges, are increasingly used for evaluation and automated content moderation. However, there is limited statistical analysis of how modifications in a rubric presented to both humans and autoraters affect their score agreement. Rubrics that ask for an overall or \emph{holistic} judgment - for example, rating the ``quality'' of an essay - may be inconsistently interpreted due to the complexity or subjectivity of the criteria. Conversely, rubrics can ask for
radar.summary_zh: 自動評分員，也稱為 LLM-as-judges，越來越多地用於評估和自動內容審核。然而，對於提供給人類和自動評分員的評分標準修改如何影響他們的評分一致性，其統計分析仍然有限。要求進行整體或 holistic 判斷的評分標準（例如，評估一篇論文的「品質」）可能由於標準的複雜性或主觀性而導致解釋不一致。
radar.title: Quantifying the Statistical Effect of Rubric Modifications on Human-Autorater Agreement
radar.title_zh: 量化評分標準修改對人類與自動評分員一致性的統計效應

links.title: Teaching LLMs Program Semantics via Symbolic Execution Traces

primary_link.title: Teaching LLMs Program Semantics via Symbolic Execution Traces

radar.summary: We introduce an evaluation framework of 500 C verification tasks across five property types (memory safety, overflow, termination, reachability, data races) built on SV-COMP 2025, and evaluate 14 models across six families. We find that high overall accuracy masks a critical weakness: while most models reliably confirm properties hold, violation detection varies widely and degrades sharply with program length. To close this gap, we train on formal verification artifacts: running the Soteria symb
radar.summary_zh: 我們提出一個基於 SV-COMP 2025 構建的評估框架，包含 500 個 C 語言驗證任務，涵蓋五種屬性類型（memory safety, overflow, termination, reachability, data races），並評估了六個家族的 14 個模型。我們發現高整體準確度掩蓋了一個關鍵弱點：儘管大多數模型能可靠地確認屬性成立，但違規檢測的差異很大，並隨著程式長度急劇下降。為了彌補這一差距，我們對形式驗證人工製品進行了訓練：運行 Soteria symb
radar.title: Teaching LLMs Program Semantics via Symbolic Execution Traces
radar.title_zh: 透過 Symbolic Execution Traces 教導 LLMs 程式語義

links.title: The Frequency Confound in Language-Model Surprisal and Metaphor Novelty

primary_link.title: The Frequency Confound in Language-Model Surprisal and Metaphor Novelty

radar.summary: Language-model (LM) surprisal is widely used as a proxy for contextual predictability and has been reported to correlate with metaphor novelty judgments. However, surprisal is tightly intertwined with lexical frequency. We explore this interaction on metaphor novelty ratings using two different word frequency measures. We analyse surprisal estimates from eight Pythia model sizes and 154 training checkpoints. Across settings, word frequency is a stronger predictor of metaphor novelty than surpris
radar.summary_zh: 語言模型（LM）驚奇度被廣泛用作上下文可預測性的代理，並據報導與隱喻新穎性判斷相關。然而，驚奇度與詞彙頻率緊密交織。我們使用兩種不同的詞頻測量方法，探討了這種交互作用對隱喻新穎性評級的影響。我們分析了來自八種 Pythia 模型尺寸和 154 個訓練檢查點的驚奇度估計。在各種設定下，詞頻是隱喻新穎性比驚奇度更強的預測因子。
radar.title: The Frequency Confound in Language-Model Surprisal and Metaphor Novelty
radar.title_zh: 語言模型驚奇度與隱喻新穎性中的頻率混淆

links.title: DINORANKCLIP: DINOv3 Distillation and Injection for Vision-Language Pretraining with High-Order Ranking Consistency

primary_link.title: DINORANKCLIP: DINOv3 Distillation and Injection for Vision-Language Pretraining with High-Order Ranking Consistency

radar.summary: Contrastive language-image pretraining (CLIP) suffers from two structural weaknesses: the symmetric InfoNCE loss discards the relative ordering among unmatched in-batch pairs, and global pooling collapses the visual representation into a semantic bottleneck that is poorly sensitive to fine-grained local structure. RANKCLIP partially addresses the first issue with a list-wise Plackett-Luce ranking-consistency loss, but its model is strictly first-order and inherits the second weakness untouched.
radar.summary_zh: 對比語言-圖像預訓練（CLIP）存在兩個結構性弱點：對稱的 InfoNCE loss 丟棄了批次內不匹配對之間的相對順序，以及全局池化將視覺表示坍塌為一個對細粒度局部結構不敏感的語義瓶頸。RANKCLIP 部分解決了第一個問題，採用了列表式的 Plackett-Luce ranking-consistency loss，但其模型嚴格來說是第一階的，並繼承了第二個弱點而未觸及。
radar.title: DINORANKCLIP: DINOv3 Distillation and Injection for Vision-Language Pretraining with High-Order Ranking Consistency
radar.title_zh: DINORANKCLIP：具備高階排序一致性的 DINOv3 蒸餾與注入用於視覺-語言預訓練

links.title: To What Extent Does Agent-generated Code Require Maintenance? An Empirical Study

primary_link.title: To What Extent Does Agent-generated Code Require Maintenance? An Empirical Study

radar.summary: LLM-based autonomous coding agents have reshaped software development. While these agents excel at code generation, open questions persist about the long-term maintainability of AI-generated code. This study empirically investigates the maintenance extent, human involvement, and modification types of AI-generated files versus human-authored code. Using the AIDev dataset of AI-generated pull requests and GitHub, we analyzed over 1,000 files and approximately 3,200 changes from 100 popular reposit
radar.summary_zh: 基於 LLM 的自主編碼代理已重塑軟體開發。儘管這些代理在程式碼生成方面表現出色，但關於 AI 生成程式碼的長期可維護性仍存在未解之題。本研究實證調查了 AI 生成檔案與人類撰寫程式碼相比的維護程度、人類參與以及修改類型。我們使用 AIDev 資料集中的 AI 生成 pull requests 和 GitHub，分析了來自 100 個熱門 repositories 的 1,000 多個檔案和約 3,200 次變更。
radar.title: To What Extent Does Agent-generated Code Require Maintenance? An Empirical Study
radar.title_zh: 代理生成程式碼在何種程度上需要維護？一項實證研究

links.title: Constraint Decay: The Fragility of LLM Agents in Backend Code Generation

primary_link.title: Constraint Decay: The Fragility of LLM Agents in Backend Code Generation

radar.summary: Large Language Model (LLM) agents demonstrate strong performance in autonomous code generation under loose specifications. However, production-grade software requires strict adherence to structural constraints, such as architectural patterns, databases, and object-relational mappings. Existing benchmarks often overlook these non-functional requirements, rewarding functionally correct but structurally arbitrary solutions. We present a systematic study evaluating how well agents handle structural
radar.summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）代理在寬鬆規範下展示了強大的自主程式碼生成能力。然而，生產級軟體需要嚴格遵守結構性約束，例如 architectural patterns, databases, 和 object-relational mappings。現有基準測試通常忽略這些非功能性要求，僅獎勵功能正確但結構隨意的解決方案。我們提出了一項系統性研究，評估代理處理結構性約束的能力。
radar.title: Constraint Decay: The Fragility of LLM Agents in Backend Code Generation
radar.title_zh: 約束衰減：LLM 代理在後端程式碼生成中的脆弱性

links.title: Proactive Instance Navigation with Comparative Judgment for Ambiguous User Queries

primary_link.title: Proactive Instance Navigation with Comparative Judgment for Ambiguous User Queries

radar.summary: Natural-language instance navigation becomes challenging when the initial user request does not uniquely specify the target instance. A practical agent should reduce the user's burden by actively asking only the information needed to distinguish the target from similar distractors, rather than requiring a detailed description upfront. Existing approaches often fall short of this goal: they may stop at the first plausible candidate before sufficiently exploring alternatives, or, even after collec
radar.summary_zh: 當初始使用者請求未能唯一指定目標實例時，自然語言 instance navigation 變得具有挑戰性。一個實用的 agent 應該透過主動詢問僅需區分目標與相似 distractors 的資訊來減輕使用者負擔，而非要求事先提供詳細描述。現有方法往往未能達到此目標：它們可能會在充分探索替代方案之前就停止在第一個看似合理的候選實例，或者，即使在收集
radar.title: Proactive Instance Navigation with Comparative Judgment for Ambiguous User Queries
radar.title_zh: 針對模糊使用者查詢的主動式實例導航結合比較判斷

links.title: RobotEQ: Transitioning from Passive Intelligence to Active Intelligence in Embodied AI

primary_link.title: RobotEQ: Transitioning from Passive Intelligence to Active Intelligence in Embodied AI

radar.summary: Embodied AI is a prominent research topic in both academia and industry. Current research centers on completing tasks based on explicit user instructions. However, for robots to integrate into human society, they must understand which actions are permissible and which are prohibited, even without explicit commands. We refer to the user-guided AI as passive intelligence and the unguided AI as active intelligence. This paper introduces RobotEQ, the first benchmark for active intelligence, aiming t
radar.summary_zh: Embodied AI 在學術界和工業界都是一個重要的研究課題。當前的研究主要集中在根據明確的使用者指令完成任務。然而，機器人若要融入人類社會，即使沒有明確指令，它們也必須理解哪些行為是允許的，哪些是禁止的。我們將使用者引導的 AI 稱為 passive intelligence，將未受引導的 AI 稱為 active intelligence。本文介紹了 RobotEQ，這是首個針對 active intelligence 的基準，旨在
radar.title: RobotEQ: Transitioning from Passive Intelligence to Active Intelligence in Embodied AI
radar.title_zh: RobotEQ：具身 AI 中從被動智慧過渡到主動智慧

links.title: Trade-off Functions for DP-SGD with Subsampling based on Random Shuffling: Tight Upper and Lower Bounds

primary_link.title: Trade-off Functions for DP-SGD with Subsampling based on Random Shuffling: Tight Upper and Lower Bounds

radar.summary: We derive a tight analysis of the trade-off function for Differentially Private Stochastic Gradient Descent (DP-SGD) with subsampling based on random shuffling within the $f$-DP framework. Our analysis covers the regime $σ\geq \sqrt{3/\ln M}$, where $σ$ is the noise multiplier and $M$ is the number of rounds within a single epoch. radar.summary_zh: 我們在 $f$-DP 框架內，對基於隨機洗牌的 subsampling 的 Differentially Private Stochastic Gradient Descent (DP-SGD) 的權衡函數進行了嚴謹的分析。我們的分析涵蓋了 $σ\geq \sqrt{3/\ln M}$ 的範圍，其中 $σ$ 是 noise multiplier，$M$ 是一個 epoch 中的輪數。與 Poisson subsampling 的 $f$-DP 分析不同，後者產生非閉合的隱式公式，可由機器計算但缺乏透明度，而隨機洗牌則允許
radar.title: Trade-off Functions for DP-SGD with Subsampling based on Random Shuffling: Tight Upper and Lower Bounds
radar.title_zh: 針對基於隨機洗牌的 Subsampling 的 DP-SGD 權衡函數：緊密上下界

links.title: Task-Aware Answer Preservation under Audio Compression for Large Audio Language Models

primary_link.title: Task-Aware Answer Preservation under Audio Compression for Large Audio Language Models

radar.summary: Large audio language models (LALMs) are increasingly used to reason over long audio clips, yet deployment often compresses audio before inference to reduce memory and latency. The risk is that compression can leave aggregate accuracy acceptable while sharply degrading answers for a deployment-critical query family. We study answer-preserving audio compression, judging a compressor by the excess answer-error it induces, especially for the worst-affected family. We formulate this theoretically as
radar.summary_zh: 大型音頻語言模型 (LALMs) 越來越多地用於對長音頻片段進行推理，然而，部署時通常會在 inference 之前壓縮音頻以減少記憶體和 latency。風險在於壓縮可能會使整體準確性尚可接受，但卻會嚴重降低針對部署關鍵查詢家族的答案品質。我們研究 answer-preserving audio compression，透過壓縮器所引起的過度答案錯誤來判斷其性能，特別是對於受影響最嚴重的家族。我們將其理論化為
radar.title: Task-Aware Answer Preservation under Audio Compression for Large Audio Language Models
radar.title_zh: 針對大型音頻語言模型的音頻壓縮下任務感知式答案保留

links.title: Sustaining Cooperation in Populations Guided by AI: A Folk Theorem for LLMs

primary_link.title: Sustaining Cooperation in Populations Guided by AI: A Folk Theorem for LLMs

radar.summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to provide instructions to many agents who interact with one another. Such shared reliance couples agents who appear to act independently: they may in fact be guided by a common model. This coupling can change the prospects for cooperation among agents with misaligned incentives. We study settings in which multiple LLMs each advise a population of clients who participate in instances of an underlying game, creating strategic interaction at the l
radar.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 越來越多地用於向許多相互作用的 agent 提供指令。這種共同依賴性將看似獨立行動的 agent 連結起來：他們實際上可能受到一個共同模型的引導。這種連結可能會改變具有不一致動機的 agent 之間合作的前景。我們研究了多個 LLMs 各自為一群客戶提供建議的場景，這些客戶參與底層遊戲的實例，從而在
radar.title: Sustaining Cooperation in Populations Guided by AI: A Folk Theorem for LLMs
radar.title_zh: 在 AI 引導的群體中維持合作：針對 LLMs 的 Folk Theorem

links.title: Overcoming reward signal challenges: Verifiable rewards-based reinforcement learning with GRPO on SageMaker AI

primary_link.title: Overcoming reward signal challenges: Verifiable rewards-based reinforcement learning with GRPO on SageMaker AI

radar.summary: In this post, you will learn how to implement reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) to introduce verification and transparency into reward signals to improve training performance. This approach works best when outputs can be objectively verified for correctness, such as in mathematical reasoning, code generation, or symbolic manipulation tasks. You will also learn how to layer techniques like Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) and few-shot examples to further improve results. You’ll use the GSM8K dataset (Grade School Math 8K: a collection of grade school math problems) to improve math problem solving accuracy, but the techniques used here can be adapted to a wide variety of other use cases.
radar.title: Overcoming reward signal challenges: Verifiable rewards-based reinforcement learning with GRPO on SageMaker AI
